title: Uncertainty from $b$ quark masses in Z pT spectrum
subtitle: Status summary
author: AlphaS from Z pT analysis team, CMS

slides.title: Overview: Samples, Goal, Methodology

items.text: Goal: build a $b$-mass uncertainty to apply to our simulation, since our nominal MiNNLO treats $b$'s as massless.
subitems: N.B.: we are already accounting for heavy quark mass effects in PDFs.
select events affected by heavy $b$ quark mass.
Build a corrected distribution as massless-$b$ MiNNLO (unselected) + massive-$b$ MiNNLO (selected).
The difference between the corrected distribution and the nominal MiNNLO is taken as an uncertainty.

items: We attempted to use the B hadrons to define the set of events that should be swapped, but it doesn't seem to work nicely. We looked at jets and LHE quarks as well, see backup.

subitems: dedicated heavy $b$ quark Z$b\bar{b}$ MiNNLO: massive $b$, 4FS. (From arXiv2404.08598: $\sigma=25.28$pb from Table 1, but unclear if that is inclusive $\ell=e,\mu$ or for each lepton flavor.)

slides.title: B-Hadron Diagnostics

top_items.text: B hadrons are identified from GenPart using PDG-ID hadron-content logic (contains bottom flavor) with GenPart status 1 or 2.
subitems: Exact implementation is shown in backup.

conclusion_items_latex: Large, unexplained normalization difference, on top of what may be a reasonable shape difference at low $p_T$.

slides.title: Swap Result and Definition

top_items.text: Swap definition: select events with at least one such $B$ hadron (no $p_T$ selection); corrected distribution is built as nominal MiNNLO (unselected) + massive-$b$ MiNNLO (selected).

conclusion_items_latex: Result: swapping gives a sizable normalization shift (not shape-only), so it is too aggressive as-is.

Questions: is this due to correct normalization difference between samples? Are we selecting more events from the nominal MiNNLO than the ones we are putting back in from the massive-$b$ MiNNLO? Or is it something else?
